what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG share icon with three circular nodes connected by two diagonal stroked paths, inheriting color from currentColor and using no fill.
observations: Root svg sets xmlns, viewBox 0 0 24 24, width 24, height 24, fill none, and stroke currentColor.
Stroke styling uses width 1.5 with round line caps and round joins.
The icon contains three circles at left center, upper right, and lower right with radius 2.5.
Two path segments connect the left node to the two right-side nodes.
standouts: Compact share/network glyph built from simple circle and line primitives.
Uses currentColor so the icon color is controlled by its context.
conventions: Follows common inline SVG icon sizing with a 24x24 viewBox.
Uses stroke-only geometry with no fill.
Uses rounded stroke terminals and joins for icon consistency.
concerns: No title, role, or aria metadata is present for standalone accessibility.
notes: A small reusable SVG asset representing a share action through connected nodes.
questions: Should accessible labeling be provided by the consuming component rather than inside this SVG?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a share icon, and the SVG content depicts a share/network symbol.
intention_signals: It appears intended to serve as a reusable share action icon that adapts to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: A scalable, color-inheriting share icon asset composed of basic SVG primitives for use in interface controls or icon sets.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 stroke-only SVG share icon with three circular nodes connected by two diagonal lines and colored via currentColor.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a share action in a reusable design-system icon asset.
